title: Cracking the TikTok Algorithm: What Actually Works in 2025
author: Kevin Chen
excerpt: The TikTok algorithm isn't a mystery — it's a system. Here's what we've learned from managing 20+ brand accounts about what the algorithm actually rewards right now.
body:
## The Algorithm Isn't Random

Let's get one thing out of the way: the TikTok algorithm is not random, and it's not unfair. It's a recommendation engine built on one metric above all — **watch time**. Every other signal (likes, shares, comments, follows) is secondary.

If people watch your video to the end, TikTok shows it to more people. Simple.

## What We've Learned From 20+ Brand Accounts

After managing TikTok for over 20 Australian brands, here's what we know works in 2025:

### 1. The First Frame Is Everything

You have less than one second to stop the scroll. The most effective hooks we've tested are mid-action openings — don't start with a title card, start with the most visually compelling moment.

### 2. Native Beats Polished

Brand accounts that post content shot on iPhones consistently outperform those posting studio-quality video. TikTok users scroll past anything that looks like an ad.

### 3. Sound Matters More Than You Think

Trending sounds still drive discovery, but original audio is gaining weight. Our best-performing client videos in Q4 2024 used a mix: trending sound for reach, original voiceover for authority.

### 4. Post Frequency > Post Quality

This is counterintuitive, but the data is clear. Accounts posting 5x per week grow 3x faster than those posting 2x per week — even if individual post quality is lower. The algorithm rewards consistency.

## The Takeaway

Stop overthinking and start posting. Film natively, hook early, use sound strategically, and show up every day. The algorithm isn't magic — it just rewards the creators who understand how people actually use the app.
